title: Fix null pointer dereference in class member trigger expressions
body:
## Summary

 Trigger expressions for `@(...)` sensitivity lists are evaluated at simulation start, even for tasks that haven't been called yet. When sensitivity expressions access class members (e.g., `@(class_handle.event)` or `@(posedge class_handle.vif.signal)`), this caused null pointer dereferences because the class handle wasn't initialized with `new()` yet.

  This fix adds null guards around trigger expression evaluation and related initialization/update statements in `V3SenExprBuilder`. The generated code now checks if the class reference is non-null before accessing its members, returning false for the trigger condition when null.

  ## Example that previously crashed

 ```systemverilog
  package my_pkg;

    class my_driver;
      virtual my_if vif;
      event my_event;
      // ...
    endclass

    task automatic my_task(my_driver drv);
      fork
        @(drv.my_event);           // Evaluated at time 0, before task called
        @(posedge drv.vif.sig);    // Also evaluated at time 0
        begin
          #10;
          -> drv.my_event;         // Trigger the event
          drv.vif.sig = 1;         // Trigger the posedge
        end
      join_any
    endtask

  endpackage

  module t;
    my_pkg::my_driver drv;

    initial begin
      drv = new(vif);  // Class constructed at time 0
      #20;
      my_pkg::my_task(drv);  // Task called later - but triggers already evaluated
    end
  endmodule
```
  ## Changes

  - Added hasClassMemberAccess() to detect class member access in expressions
  - Added getBaseClassRef() to find the outermost class reference needing null-check
  - Added wrapStmtWithNullCheck() and wrapExprWithNullCheck() helpers
  - Applied null guards to pre-updates, post-updates, initializers, and trigger expressions
  - Added regression test t_class_trigger_null.v

  ## Regression test

  t_class_trigger_null.v exercises three trigger patterns:
  1. Class member event - @(h.my_event) - waits on an event declared inside a class
  2. Posedge through virtual interface - @(posedge h.vif.sig) - edge detection on a signal accessed via class member virtual interface
  3. Negedge through virtual interface - @(negedge h.vif.sig) - same pattern with negative edge

  The test constructs the class at time 0, then calls the task at time 20. Before this fix, Verilator would crash with a null pointer dereference during elaboration because trigger expressions were evaluated before the task was ever called.

  The test verifies that:
  - Simulation completes without crashing
  - All three trigger types fire exactly once
  - The triggers respond correctly to their respective stimuli (event trigger, signal posedge, signal negedge)
